title: Proactive tail harmonizer and cohort splitter for longitudinal benchmarks
preview: Context and motivation: Longitudinal benchmark runs at Redwood have shown unstable tail behavior when cohorts drift across multiple phases (warmup -> steady -> degraded). This PR introduces a lightweight 'Proactive Tail Harmonizer' that proactively detects cohort drift and a deterministic 'Cohort Splitter' that part...
text:
Context and motivation: Longitudinal benchmark runs at Redwood have shown unstable tail behavior when cohorts drift across multiple phases (warmup -> steady -> degraded). This PR introduces a lightweight 'Proactive Tail Harmonizer' that proactively detects cohort drift and a deterministic 'Cohort Splitter' that partitions sessions into stable cohorts for more reproducible long-tail quantiles. The goal is to reduce p99/p999 variance across repeated runs and make performance changes easier to attribute to code or config changes.

What this PR delivers (summary): 1) An in-process harmonizer module that computes cohort fingerprints over sliding windows and applies a corrective reweighting when cohort composition shifts by >5% for the tail cohort. 2) A deterministic cohort splitter that marks sessions into stable buckets based on request size distribution, KV cache hit patterns, and primer...

title: KernelTide contention visualizer and probabilistic warmup coordinator for stability-focused bench suites
preview: Motivation: We've observed non-deterministic p95/p99 spikes in a subset of long-running throughput benchmarks that correlate with kernel-level contention and uneven warmup behavior across workers. This PR introduces two complementary components: (1) KernelTide — a contention visualizer that aggregates per-kernel occ...
text:
Motivation: We've observed non-deterministic p95/p99 spikes in a subset of long-running throughput benchmarks that correlate with kernel-level contention and uneven warmup behavior across workers. This PR introduces two complementary components: (1) KernelTide — a contention visualizer that aggregates per-kernel occupancy, stall-reasons and scheduling collisions into time-aligned heatmaps and compact contention-curves; (2) ProbabilisticWarmupCoordinator — a controller that runs randomized preflight primer passes across cohorts to probabilistically cover different KV cache and memory-placement states before measurement windows.

What this PR does (summary): Adds a lightweight sampling probe that annotates kernel events in the benchmark worker (Linux perf + runtime hooks), a postprocessor that synthesizes per-request kernel timelines into a compact parquet-friendly schema, an interactiv...

title: phase-shifted-latency-sweep-and-coldstart-prober
preview: Motivation: We need a lightweight, reproducible runner that exercises both steady-state and phase-shifted burst patterns while simultaneously measuring cold-start latency across different model variants and runtime kernel configs. This PR introduces a phase-shifted latency sweep runner and a paired cold-start prober...
text:
Motivation: We need a lightweight, reproducible runner that exercises both steady-state and phase-shifted burst patterns while simultaneously measuring cold-start latency across different model variants and runtime kernel configs. This PR introduces a phase-shifted latency sweep runner and a paired cold-start prober that can be composed to produce joint distributions (latency vs cold-start tail) used by the regression detector pipeline.
Design:
- phase_sweep_runner: Python runner that generates a sequence of workload phases with configurable duration, RPS, sequence length distribution and an adjustable phase-offset parameter to produce controlled interleaving across client shards.
- coldstart_prober: small Go probe that issues warmup-less single-shot requests on a configurable model selection (quantized/FP16/FP32) to capture cold-start percentiles under concurrent load.
- reporter bri...

title: Add GPU thermal-throttle emulator and warmup stability harness
preview: Motivation: Intermittent production incidents traced to GPU thermal/power throttling during long inference bursts caused rare regressions in tail latency and kernel scheduling. We need a reproducible, low-overhead emulator to inject thermal/power profiles into benchmark runs and a repeatable warmup-stability harness...
text:
Motivation: Intermittent production incidents traced to GPU thermal/power throttling during long inference bursts caused rare regressions in tail latency and kernel scheduling. We need a reproducible, low-overhead emulator to inject thermal/power profiles into benchmark runs and a repeatable warmup-stability harness to measure time-to-steady-state under those conditions. This PR introduces a GPU thermal-throttle emulator and a warmup stability harness that: 1) simulates frequency/power capping curves, 2) injects transient throttles at configurable epochs, 3) coordinates with the existing warmup priming runner to capture time-to-convergence and tail variance, and 4) writes compact stability artifacts for downstream analysis.

What changed (high level): - New thermal_emulator/ module (Python) that runs an in-process state machine describing temperature/power curves and emits a small shi...

title: Introduce latency-distribution-collector and histogram-exporter for perf runs
preview: Motivation: our current perf suite reports mean/median p50/p95/p99 but lacks a first-class histogram distribution export that lets ops and product slices examine tail behavior by bucket and request class. This PR introduces a latency-distribution-collector (benchmarks/runner/latency_collector.py) and a histogram_exp...
text: Motivation: our current perf suite reports mean/median p50/p95/p99 but lacks a first-class histogram distribution export that lets ops and product slices examine tail behavior by bucket and request class. This PR introduces a latency-distribution-collector (benchmarks/runner/latency_collector.py) and a histogram_exporter that emits fixed-window histograms and a Prometheus-compatible scrape endpoint for aggregated CI runs. Key changes: 1) adds a thread-safe latency_collector that supports labeled buckets (model, route, sequence_length_bin) and controlled flush intervals; 2) implements histogram_exporter with configurable bucket boundaries and JSON/Prometheus output modes; 3) integrates collection step into ci/perf-pipeline.yml so nightly jobs persist artifacts to the perf artifacts store; 4) adds validation tests and an examples script to reproduce locally. Implementation notes: the co...

title: introduce kernel-variance prober to map per-layer sequence-length inefficiencies
preview: Motivation: We frequently see tail latency regressions that only appear for particular sequence-length distributions. Existing workload runners produce aggregate token-level metrics but do not attribute variance to model kernels or layers. This PR adds a 'kernel-variance prober' that runs focused microbenchmarks acr...
text: Motivation: We frequently see tail latency regressions that only appear for particular sequence-length distributions. Existing workload runners produce aggregate token-level metrics but do not attribute variance to model kernels or layers. This PR adds a 'kernel-variance prober' that runs focused microbenchmarks across sequence length windows and collects fine-grained per-kernel timing and variance profiles so we can prioritize kernel/graph-level optimizations and targeted quantization tradeoffs. Change summary: - New binary tools/bench/prober/kernel_variance_prober.py: orchestrates probing campaigns across sequence-length buckets, random seeds, and batch shapes; supports warmup/steady-state detection and adaptive sampling. - Benchmark harness extensions bench/harness/sequence_sampler.py: sequence-length aware samplers and an API to plug sampling policies into existing runners. - Prof...

title: Epochal hotpath bisector and phase-stress harness for p95 reproduction
preview: Motivation: We've seen recurring intermittent p95 regressions across long-running multi-epoch workloads where the signal only appears after many warmup cycles. Root-cause is often a narrow commit window interacting with kernel scheduler behaviour and warmup timing. This PR introduces two complementary tools: (1) an ...
text:
Motivation: We've seen recurring intermittent p95 regressions across long-running multi-epoch workloads where the signal only appears after many warmup cycles. Root-cause is often a narrow commit window interacting with kernel scheduler behaviour and warmup timing. This PR introduces two complementary tools: (1) an epochal hotpath bisector that runs compact workload epochs across commit ranges to quickly narrow offending commits, and (2) a phase-stress harness that injects controlled scheduling/jitter/prefetch delays into the runtime to reliably amplify the rare p95 event so it becomes reproducible in CI/local benches.

What this change does: Implements a bisector CLI that (a) shards an epochal workload into deterministic micro-epochs, (b) runs them across a git commit range with parallel workers and conservative resource isolation, and (c) aggregates per-epoch phase-bucket histograms...

title: Add quant profile rollback benchmarks (latency + quality) and CI artifact export
preview:
Title: Add quant profile rollback benchmarks (latency + quality) and CI artifact export

Context
We’ve had a few regressions during quant profile experiments where we needed to confirm that a rollback actually restores baseline behavior (both performance and quality) before unpausing a rollout. This PR adds first-cl...
text:
Title: Add quant profile rollback benchmarks (latency + quality) and CI artifact export

Context
We’ve had a few regressions during quant profile experiments where we needed to confirm that a rollback actually restores baseline behavior (both performance and quality) before unpausing a rollout. This PR adds first-class benchmark scenarios to benchmark-lab that (1) compare two quant profile versions head-to-head and (2) validate that a rollback from candidate -> baseline produces results within defined tolerances.

What’s in this PR
- New benchmark suite: quant_profile_rollback
 - Runs A/B comparisons for two quant profile versions (e.g., qp_v12 vs qp_v11) on the same prompt distribution and concurrency.
 - Runs an explicit rollback scenario: baseline -> candidate -> baseline, ensuring the second baseline run matches the first baseline run (within noise thresholds).
- Metrics captured
...

title: Generate perf-canary baselines from controlled prod-like load runs
preview: Summary: This PR adds a repeatable baseline generator job in benchmark-lab that computes per-model performance baseline distributions from a controlled prod-like load run. The output artifacts are written in a stable, versioned format that perf-canary can consume for regression evaluation (min samples + percent-chan...
text: Summary: This PR adds a repeatable baseline generator job in benchmark-lab that computes per-model performance baseline distributions from a controlled prod-like load run. The output artifacts are written in a stable, versioned format that perf-canary can consume for regression evaluation (min samples + percent-change thresholds). Motivation: perf-canary needs a durable baseline per (model, model_version, runtime_build, hardware_sku, region) that reflects real scheduler behavior (queueing/batching) without using customer traffic. Early experiments were using ad-hoc queries on ephemeral metrics, which made it hard to reproduce baselines and triggered baseline drift/cardinality issues during rollout. This job makes baselines an explicit artifact with an audit trail and a consistent schema. Scope / What’s included: (1) New job `baselinegen` that ingests a benchmark-lab load-run result bu...

title: Embeddings: add high-QPS mode + throughput-first report + percentile latency export
preview: Summary: Adds an embeddings workload mode designed for high-QPS / small-payload traffic shapes and a throughput-first reporting path (req/s + embeddings/s, plus tokens/s where applicable). Also exports percentile latency (p50/p95/p99) in machine-readable form for perf-canary + dashboard ingestion. This is needed for...
text:
Summary: Adds an embeddings workload mode designed for high-QPS / small-payload traffic shapes and a throughput-first reporting path (req/s + embeddings/s, plus tokens/s where applicable). Also exports percentile latency (p50/p95/p99) in machine-readable form for perf-canary + dashboard ingestion. This is needed for Q1 2026 baseline sweeps because embeddings traffic is dominated by concurrency/queueing behavior and req/s saturation, and the existing generator/reporting was text-generation-centric (TTFT/TPOT) and didn’t provide stable throughput/saturation indicators.

Motivation / context: Our representative workload suite v1 includes an embeddings cohort with production-like payload sizes and concurrency targets (high QPS, small input lengths, mostly non-streaming). For baseline comparability we need: (1) a generator that can drive stable, controlled offered load (open-loop) while st...

title: Add long-context prefill workload + standardized warmup to reduce variance
preview:
Title: Add long-context prefill workload + standardized warmup to reduce variance

Context
We need a production-representative workload that stresses prefill (large prompt / long context) with modest decode, and we need more repeatable runs for the Q1 2026 baseline sweeps. In early dry runs we saw high variance driv...
text:
Title: Add long-context prefill workload + standardized warmup to reduce variance

Context
We need a production-representative workload that stresses prefill (large prompt / long context) with modest decode, and we need more repeatable runs for the Q1 2026 baseline sweeps. In early dry runs we saw high variance driven by (a) first-run JIT + memory pool ramp, (b) GPU clocks/ramp state, and (c) caching artifacts depending on prior suite order. This PR introduces (1) a long-context prefill workload generator and (2) a standardized warmup phase that is applied consistently across workloads (opt-in in config, default-on for long_context_prefill).

What’s in this PR
1) New workload: long_context_prefill
- Goal: mimic long-context summarization / doc QA traffic: large prefill, smaller decode.
- Shape:
 - prompt length bins (tokens): 8k / 16k / 32k (configurable; default mix 0.45/0.45/0.10)
 ...

title: Implement chat streaming workload generator (request mix, concurrency ramp, token pacing) + TTFT/TPOT export
preview:
Motivation: We need a production-representative chat streaming workload for the Q1 2026 perf baseline & suite. Existing harness coverage is request/response (non-streaming) only, which under-measures scheduling + queueing dynamics and makes TTFT/TPOT comparisons noisy across client implementations.

What this adds: ...
text:
Motivation: We need a production-representative chat streaming workload for the Q1 2026 perf baseline & suite. Existing harness coverage is request/response (non-streaming) only, which under-measures scheduling + queueing dynamics and makes TTFT/TPOT comparisons noisy across client implementations.

What this adds: (1) A chat streaming workload generator (server SSE / chunked streaming) with configurable request-mix, sequence-length bins, and streaming cadence; (2) a concurrency ramp runner (warmup + stepped load) to mimic bursty traffic and reduce variance; (3) standardized baseline outputs for TTFT + TPOT with explicit measurement boundaries (client-side network excluded as much as practical).

Key behaviors: Request mix is modeled as weighted "cohorts" (e.g., short prompt + short decode, short prompt + long decode, medium prompt + medium decode) with configurable distributions. Con...

title: Add canonical run manifest JSON schema + validator; ship example manifests; add CI to prevent drift
preview: Context: For the Q1'26 Perf baseline & representative workload suite we need results to be reproducible and attributable across runs, regions, GPU SKUs, and scheduler/kernel changes. Today our benchmark harnesses emit slightly different “metadata blobs”, which makes run comparison brittle and blocks automated gating...
text: Context: For the Q1'26 Perf baseline & representative workload suite we need results to be reproducible and attributable across runs, regions, GPU SKUs, and scheduler/kernel changes. Today our benchmark harnesses emit slightly different “metadata blobs”, which makes run comparison brittle and blocks automated gating. This PR introduces the canonical “run manifest” format and enforcement tooling per ADR-0172 (benchmark metrics + run manifest schema). What’s in this PR: (1) JSON Schema for the canonical run manifest (schema id + semver) covering environment capture (driver/CUDA, OS image, container digest), runtime build identifiers, model identifiers/config, scheduler config pointers, workload definition + seed, run mode (streaming/non-streaming), and required tags (region, sku, cohort). (2) A small validator CLI that can be used locally and in CI to validate manifests (and optionally ...
links: Linear: ENG-4822 (Run manifest and results schema standardization)
Project: Q1 2026 Perf baseline & representative workload suite

title: introduce-cohort-emulator-with-flame-segmentation-reporter
preview: Context: We often see p95/p99 spikes in the benchmark lab but lack a compact, deterministic way to map tail episodes to kernel-level hotspots across cohorts. This PR adds a lightweight cohort emulator that replays compact multi-session cohorts with controlled timing and a new flame-segmentation reporter that emits c...
text: Context: We often see p95/p99 spikes in the benchmark lab but lack a compact, deterministic way to map tail episodes to kernel-level hotspots across cohorts. This PR adds a lightweight cohort emulator that replays compact multi-session cohorts with controlled timing and a new flame-segmentation reporter that emits compact 'flame segment' artifacts for downstream attribution and visualization. Motivation: Make it possible to reproduce microburst segments deterministically, compare them against a golden set, and quickly iterate on kernel/serving changes without full cluster replays. Scope: emulator core, segmentation reporter, goldset generator, CI perf job integration, docs and an example cohort. Checklist: - [x] Emulator core with deterministic seed and time dilation - [x] Flame-segmentation reporter that emits compact JSON segments and optional pprof links - [x] Goldset generator and...

title: Introduce simultaneous prefetch stress scheduler and OOM spectra reporter
preview: Motivation: We observed correlated OOMs and long-tail latency spikes when many concurrent inferences attempted identical prefix prefetching/pinning at the same time. Small-scale reproductions masked the issue because they serialized prefetch. This PR introduces a synthetic scheduler that reproduces simultaneous pref...
text:
Motivation: We observed correlated OOMs and long-tail latency spikes when many concurrent inferences attempted identical prefix prefetching/pinning at the same time. Small-scale reproductions masked the issue because they serialized prefetch. This PR introduces a synthetic scheduler that reproduces simultaneous prefetch pressure at scale and a compact OOM 'spectra' reporter that summarizes allocation patterns leading to crashes.

What this change does: Adds a new workload runner (sim-prefetch-stressor) that can spawn thousands of virtual sessions with configurable prefetch windows, KV-cache priming patterns, and stagger jitter; the runner can optionally inject hold-until-commit semantics to simulate long-lived memory pinning. It also adds oom-spectra-reporter, a post-process tool that consumes core dumps/heap snapshots and produces an epochal histogram of allocation-site families, pea...

title: staggered-concurrency-simulator-and-kernel-affinity-replayer
preview: Motivation: Our existing workload runners produce steady-state and bursty traffic, but they don't model situations where multiple client cohorts arrive in staggered waves with different concurrency profiles and pinned kernel/NUMA affinities. This PR introduces a new simulator and reporting pipeline that: 1) generate...
text:
Motivation: Our existing workload runners produce steady-state and bursty traffic, but they don't model situations where multiple client cohorts arrive in staggered waves with different concurrency profiles and pinned kernel/NUMA affinities. This PR introduces a new simulator and reporting pipeline that: 1) generates multi-cohort, staggered-concurrency workloads (wave-based arrival) with configurable inter-wave gaps and tail-heavy sub-cohorts; 2) models kernel affinity and scheduler-level contention across heterogeneous serving nodes (GPU+CPU mixes) so we can attribute latency spikes to kernel-level queuing vs runtime scheduling; 3) outputs compact heatmaps and per-kernel flamepoint snapshots compatible with our telemetry exporter so downstream dashboards can surface contention hotspots and affinity misconfigurations.

What this PR changes (high level): - benchmark-lab/sim/staggered_s...

title: Export benchmark results as versioned CSV/JSON artifacts for web ingestion (schema + changelog)
preview: Context: Marketing site + interactive benchmark table needs a stable, versioned export of benchmark results that can be ingested by the web build pipeline and served via the results service (ENG-18566). This PR adds an export step that materializes benchmark outputs into (1) a canonical JSON bundle and (2) a CSV for...
text:
Context: Marketing site + interactive benchmark table needs a stable, versioned export of benchmark results that can be ingested by the web build pipeline and served via the results service (ENG-18566). This PR adds an export step that materializes benchmark outputs into (1) a canonical JSON bundle and (2) a CSV for quick inspection / spreadsheet workflows. Both artifacts are versioned, have an explicit schema version, and include a machine-readable changelog so the docs/web layer can safely detect and adapt to changes.

What’s included: - New exporter module (export/public_artifacts) that takes validated run aggregates and emits: - artifacts/public/v1/results.json (canonical) - artifacts/public/v1/results.csv (flat, human-friendly) - artifacts/public/v1/changelog.json (schema + field-level changes) - artifacts/public/v1/manifest.json (build metadata: git sha, created_at, prompt_set, ...

title: Stability gates: auto-rerun on p95 variance threshold + flaky-run CI coverage
preview: Context: We’re seeing run-to-run instability in p95 latency on some model/region pairs (queueing effects, transient noisy neighbors, kernel selection differences). This PR adds a stability gate to the benchmark pipeline that detects high variance in p95 latency and automatically re-runs the affected slice up to a bo...
text: Context: We’re seeing run-to-run instability in p95 latency on some model/region pairs (queueing effects, transient noisy neighbors, kernel selection differences). This PR adds a stability gate to the benchmark pipeline that detects high variance in p95 latency and automatically re-runs the affected slice up to a bounded limit. Goal: reduce noise in published aggregates while preserving the raw evidence trail (we keep the original run results and annotate what happened). What’s included: (1) Variance detection on repeated trials for each (model, region, workload, concurrency) slice, based on p95 latency. (2) Auto rerun when variance exceeds a configurable threshold, with a cap on reruns and explicit status reporting. (3) Manifest + result artifacts include stability metadata (variance %, trials, rerun_count, stability_status, stability_notes). (4) CI coverage: simulated flaky runs wit...

title: Publish baseline vs quant-profile cost/token + latency deltas (CSV summaries + artifacts)
preview: Context: As part of the Quantization Profile Library rollout, we need a repeatable, reviewable set of benchmark outputs that quantify the impact of enabling a published quantization profile vs baseline precision on (a) unit economics (approx cost/token) and (b) latency/throughput across supported GPU SKUs. This PR a...
text:
Context: As part of the Quantization Profile Library rollout, we need a repeatable, reviewable set of benchmark outputs that quantify the impact of enabling a published quantization profile vs baseline precision on (a) unit economics (approx cost/token) and (b) latency/throughput across supported GPU SKUs. This PR adds a small publishing pipeline in benchmark-lab that generates normalized CSV summaries per model/profile/GPU and uploads both raw run logs and the rolled-up tables as CI artifacts. The goal is to let Applied ML + Runtime + Platform point to a single source of truth for “expected deltas” used by Console previews, rollout gates, and incident triage.

What’s included: (1) A new runner entrypoint that executes baseline + profile-enabled scenarios back-to-back with consistent warmup and pinned runtime build/commit; (2) CSV summaries under artifacts/quant_profiles/ summarizing ...

title: introduce-mini-deterministic-benchmark-corpus-and-sparse-cpu-probe-reporter
preview: Motivation: We lack a compact, deterministic benchmark fixture that reproduces common short-sequence hotpaths (latency-sensitive generations of length 8-64 tokens) while being cheap to run in CI and on local dev machines. Current suites are heavyweight and noisy; this PR introduces a curated mini corpus plus an expe...
text:
Motivation: We lack a compact, deterministic benchmark fixture that reproduces common short-sequence hotpaths (latency-sensitive generations of length 8-64 tokens) while being cheap to run in CI and on local dev machines. Current suites are heavyweight and noisy; this PR introduces a curated mini corpus plus an experimental sparse CPU probe reporter that samples kernel-level wall-clock for a small set of annotated hotspots. The goal is faster benches that still exercise scheduling, KV-cache priming, and transient batch assembly so we can catch regressions early.

What this PR contains (high-level):
- mini_bench_corpus/: a compact set (12 sessions) of synthetic and real prompt traces sampled from production anonymized logs, normalized for determinism (fixed tokenization seeds, pinned random seeds, and controlled primer lengths).
- runner/mini_runner.py: lightweight harness that spins u...

title: Introduce timebox seeder and hierarchical telemetry compactifier for long-run benchmarks
preview: Motivation: Long-duration benchmark runs in Benchmark Lab are noisy and expensive to store because we capture full-event traces and per-sample payloads for every iteration. Two common pain points: (1) realistic warm-cache steady-state is expensive to reach deterministically across different sequence-length mixes; (2...
text:
Motivation: Long-duration benchmark runs in Benchmark Lab are noisy and expensive to store because we capture full-event traces and per-sample payloads for every iteration. Two common pain points: (1) realistic warm-cache steady-state is expensive to reach deterministically across different sequence-length mixes; (2) raw telemetry volume for long runs (multi-hour stress / soak) is large, which slows downstream analysis and inflates storage costs.

Design & changes: Introduce a timebox seeder (timebox-seeder) that drives a configurable, bounded priming stage prior to each measurement epoch. The seeder supports: target distributions (seq-length, token-priming prefixes, model-mix), controlled eviction pressure knobs, and deterministic seeding modes (seed schedules can be reused across runs for reproducibility). Parallel to the seeder, add a hierarchical telemetry compactifier (rollup-tel...

title: holo-surge-sequencer-and-crossframe-occupancy-exporter
preview: Motivation: we observed sustained mid-tail p95 instability when replaying multi-sequence conversational traffic with interleaved priming and varied KV cache hits. Existing synthetic runners produce either Poisson-like bursts or deterministic ramps; they do not emulate the fractal-like, multi-scale surges we see in p...
text:
Motivation: we observed sustained mid-tail p95 instability when replaying multi-sequence conversational traffic with interleaved priming and varied KV cache hits. Existing synthetic runners produce either Poisson-like bursts or deterministic ramps; they do not emulate the fractal-like, multi-scale surges we see in production that cause cross-frame resource contention and transient kernel occupancy spikes. This PR introduces the 'holo-surge' sequencer (a compact workload generator that composes deterministic priming, stochastic microbursts, and long-period envelope modulation) and a crossframe occupancy exporter that records per-step GPU occupancy signatures and correlates them with logical request frames for downstream reporters.

What this change does: adds a new sequencer (cmd/holo-surge) and a reporting pipeline that serializes occupancy traces in a compact delta format compatible ...

title: Steady-state slew-rate prober and latency-heatmap serializer
preview: Context: Production traffic exhibits sudden arrival-rate changes that current benchmarks treat as either isolated spikes or steady-state; we lacked a lightweight prober to characterize slew (rate-of-change) and to mark steady-state windows for downstream aggregations. This PR introduces a 'slew-rate prober' that emi...
text: Context: Production traffic exhibits sudden arrival-rate changes that current benchmarks treat as either isolated spikes or steady-state; we lacked a lightweight prober to characterize slew (rate-of-change) and to mark steady-state windows for downstream aggregations. This PR introduces a 'slew-rate prober' that emits time-series markers and a compact latency-heatmap serializer to efficiently export per-window histograms for postprocessing. Motivation: Provide an automated way to (1) detect sustained ramp-up/down phases, (2) isolate true steady-state windows for p95/p99 comparison across builds, and (3) export telemetry in a bandwidth-efficient format so long-run batch analyses are cheaper. High-level changes: added a new python prober harness (slew_rate_prober.py) with a configurable EWMA-based slew estimator and a hysteresis-driven steady-state detector; implemented adaptive samplin...

title: Phase-locked traffic replayer and quantile-hashmap serializer
preview: Motivation: We frequently observe ephemeral microbursts that escape our existing replayers because sequence timing jitter masks phase relationships across concurrent sessions. This PR introduces a phase-locked loop (PLL) inspired traffic replayer that aligns request phases across synthetic sessions to deterministica...
text:
Motivation: We frequently observe ephemeral microbursts that escape our existing replayers because sequence timing jitter masks phase relationships across concurrent sessions. This PR introduces a phase-locked loop (PLL) inspired traffic replayer that aligns request phases across synthetic sessions to deterministically reproduce multi-tenant interference patterns and kernel saturation events. It also adds a compact quantile-hashmap serializer for per-epoch quantile sketches so reporting pipelines can stream delta-updates without holding large in-memory sketches.

What this PR does (single-string summary): Adds a new lightweight replayer (bench/replayers/phase_locked_replayer.py) that can lock multiple synthetic sessions to a scheduler phase, implements deterministic seeding and jitter maps, exposes an API to pin phase offsets and slot widths, and integrates with the existing bench-run...

title: sequence churn stressor and multiwindow latency mapper
preview: Context: We need a compact runner and reporting pipeline that exercises 'sequence churn'—the interaction between variable context window sizes, primer reuse, and short-lived KV cache entries that manifest as multi-window latency regressions in production. Motivation: Recent production traces showed p95 regressions c...
text: Context: We need a compact runner and reporting pipeline that exercises 'sequence churn'—the interaction between variable context window sizes, primer reuse, and short-lived KV cache entries that manifest as multi-window latency regressions in production. Motivation: Recent production traces showed p95 regressions correlated with bursts of short-context requests that evict hot KV entries, causing downstream longer-context requests to suffer. This PR introduces a workload generator (seq-churn-stressor) and a reporting component (multiwindow-latency-mapper) that: 1) reproduces realistic churn patterns across tenants and primer-length distributions, 2) collects per-window latency heatmaps (window sizes: 32, 64, 128, 256 tokens), 3) emits cohorted quantiles and an artifact CSV for cross-run comparison. Checklist: - [x] Implement stochastic primer/seed generator with tenant skew and TTL-dr...

title: asymmetric-concurrency-skew-generator-and-delta-reporter
preview: Motivation: production traffic often exhibits heavy user skew where a small fraction of sessions produce high token bursts and long-lived KV cache pressure. Existing synthetic runners in benchmark-lab model uniform sequence mixes or simple hot-spot priors; we need a compact harness that can: 1) generate temporally c...
text:
Motivation: production traffic often exhibits heavy user skew where a small fraction of sessions produce high token bursts and long-lived KV cache pressure. Existing synthetic runners in benchmark-lab model uniform sequence mixes or simple hot-spot priors; we need a compact harness that can: 1) generate temporally correlated bursty sessions with configurable skew parameters; 2) drive the serving runtime under asymmetric concurrency to exercise scheduling and KV cache eviction behaviors; and 3) produce a compact delta report that correlates latency/regression deltas with kernel-level sample points and concurrency slices so that regressions can be triaged to scheduling vs kernel choices.

This PR implements: a) an "asymmetric-concurrency-skew" workload generator (bench/chaos/asymmetric_skew.go + python harness) supporting: percentiles-of-sessions that become "bursters", configurable bur...
